title: Add label! filter to container filters
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
No


```release-note
Add label! filter to containers and pods filters
```

comments.body: can anyone tell me if the CI server is ok?

Thank you for your PR! CI has passed -- the blue-icon "rpm-build" jobs failed, but those fail very often so we just ignore them (they are not needed for merging).

Broadly, I'm a little uncomfortable with the implementation (I would prefer to see a more general-purpose mechanism for negative tests) but I don't know how that could be done; maybe others on the team can suggest ideas.

The test could use some work: right now it's only a no-error-thrown test, not an actual functionality test. I will take a closer look at that code tomorrow, during my workday, and see if I can come up with ideas on that.

Thanks again!

Strange that we have podman pod ps --filter label!...  But not podman ps.
Code LGTM, but additional tests would be good, Also did you look at the podman pod ps implementation?

@rhatdan no i dont and it does not
```
xtex@xtex ~> podman pod ls -f=label!=app
Error: label! is an invalid filter
xtex@xtex ~ [125]> podman pod ls -f=label=app
POD ID        NAME        STATUS      CREATED      INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
6ff0ef1111a9  caddy       Exited      3 hours ago  aacac6773972  2
```
the docs is just like to be copied from `network-ls`.
I have updated the style of documentation to keep synchronized with other pages(`network-ls` and `volume-ls`, etc.)

@edsantiago I triggered a rebuild with a empty commit (:
before that, almost all builds (except rpm-builds) on CirrusCI are failed with a Java ExecutorService exception from CirrusCI

@xtexChooser could you please apply the following diff? This will actually test your added functionality. It also adds a test for `label=`, which apparently we've never had (the existing test code was pretty much a NOP).

Also, please squash your commits before re-pushing.

Thank you!

```diff
diff --git a/test/apiv2/20-containers.at b/test/apiv2/20-containers.at
index f71aae0b5..6906273fe 100644
--- a/test/apiv2/20-containers.at
+++ b/test/apiv2/20-containers.at
@@ -409,6 +409,9 @@ t GET containers/json 200 \
 podman stop bar
 
 #compat api list containers sanity checks
+podman run -d --rm --name labelcontainer_with $ENV_WORKDIR_IMG top
+podman run -d --rm --name labelcontainer_without $IMAGE top
+
 t GET containers/json?filters='garb1age}' 500 \
     .cause="invalid character 'g' looking for beginning of value"
 t GET containers/json?filters='{"label":["testl' 500 \
@@ -436,8 +439,15 @@ t POST containers/prune?filters='{"label":["tes' 500 \
 t POST libpod/containers/prune?filters='{"label":["tes' 500 \
     .cause="unexpected end of JSON input"
 
-t GET libpod/containers/json?filters='{"label":["testlabel"]}' 200 length=0
-t GET libpod/containers/json?filters='{"label!":["testlabel"]}' 200 length=0
+t GET libpod/containers/json?filters='{"label":["created_by"]}' 200 \
+  length=1 \
+  .[0].Names[0]="labelcontainer_with"
+t GET libpod/containers/json?filters='{"label!":["created_by"]}' 200 \
+  length=1 \
+  .[0].Names[0]="labelcontainer_without"
+t GET libpod/containers/json?filters='{"label!":["testlabel"]}' 200 length=2
+
+podman stop -t0 labelcontainer_with labelcontainer_without
 
 # libpod api: do not use list filters for prune
 t POST libpod/containers/prune?filters='{"name":["anyname"]}' 500 \
```
Thank you! Could you please fix the commit message too?
`git commit --amend -s`, and remove the extraneous cruft.

One doc nit, the rest LGTM
You also have trailing white space(s) somewhere, I've used `sed -i 's/\s*$//g' file_name ` in the past to make them go away.

/lgtm
/hold
Thanks @xtexChooser 
If the tests pass, feel free to cancel the hold.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *xtexChooser* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

sry. I just fixed the python tests.

also I am sure that the old test in 6ecdf4c389fd8e079fbfae76c11351c0301eb003 is broken as `=` is URL-encoded.
